title: neoalsomitra simplex
intro: Neoalsomitra simplex, also known as the false ariseme or false dragon ariseme, is a type of flowering plant in the family Araliaceae. It is traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, which are attributed to the presence of saponins and other bioactive compounds in its roots and stems. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, and is also valued for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. Neoalsomitra simplex can be prepared for medicinal use through various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often made from the roots and stems of the plant.
